question: What specific events could re-rate or further de-rate NVO in the next 6 months?

findings.claim: Semaglutide compound patent ('122) expired March 20, 2026 — but key US patent ('343) extends to December 5, 2031 due to PTA/PTE. US generics unlikely before 2031-2033.
findings.data: Two key compound patents: US Patent 8,129,343 (expires Dec 5, 2031 with extensions) and US Patent 8,536,122 (expired March 20, 2026). Novo holds 29 total patents on semaglutide in US, some extending to 2033-2035. 49 granted follow-on patents extend protection to 2042. Semaglutide is a peptide biologic requiring biosimilar pathway ($100-300M, 5-8 years). Earliest realistic US generic: 2032-2033. Viatris won a patent litigation victory July 22, 2025, potentially accelerating biosimilar to before 2032.

findings.claim: International patent expiries already happening: India, Canada, China, Brazil all seeing generic/biosimilar semaglutide in 2026.
findings.data: Canada: generic launches expected Jan 2026, Sandoz using Canada as launchpad. India: Lupin developing injectable+tablet forms for 2026 launch. Indian generics already pricing 70% below Novo (news March 23, 2026). Brazil: Supreme Court refused patent extension, expiry March 2026. China: patent expiring 2026, multiple domestic companies in late-stage trials. These markets = ~40% of world population, ~33% of adults with obesity. Canadian prices could drop 50-80%.

findings.claim: CATALYST (DE-RATE within 6 months): India generic wave is live NOW (March 2026). Prices already 70% below Novo. Canada generics imminent. These won't kill US revenue but will compress International Operations growth.
findings.data: Benzinga (March 23, 2026): 'India's drugmakers roll out low-cost copies for Novo Nordisk's semaglutide, slashing prices up to 70%.' International Operations was DKK 112B in 2024 under Doustdar's leadership. Patent-related LOE in these markets directly impacts the International segment that was NVO's growth engine.
